column1: # Phase 3 mouse support: mid-entry cell-reference splicing on click.
# Typing a partial formula and clicking a cell splices that cell's
# address into the buffer — but only when the buffer is in a
# cell-ref-accepting position (empty, or ending in an operator,
# paren, comma, dot, comparison, or logical token). Clicking
# while the buffer ends in a digit, close-paren, or other non-
# operator char is a safe no-op.
# Seed A1 = 10, C3 = 5 so the committed formula has values to sum.
# Back to A2 so the entry has somewhere to go that references A1+C3.
# Click A1 at (x=7, y=5) — splices A1 into the buffer.
# Clicking again after a close-paren would be nonsense. Append `)`
# then verify a click at A1 is a no-op (buffer unchanged).
# First wrap the whole expression: move cursor back? The current
# implementation is append-only, so put a `)` at the end as a probe.
# Backspace the trailing `)` back off (simulate editing) and
# commit the formula. The committed cell holds the expected sum.
# F2 to EDIT, append `+`, click D4 (empty), Enter. Result unchanged
# numerically (D4 defaults to 0) but buffer splicing still works.
